weather: Sunny and mild today in Edmonds—62 °F under crystal-clear skies, zero rain chance. Northwest breeze 10-15 mph keeps it fresh. Evening stays clear, cooling to the low-40s with a light northeast wind. Perfect for a stroller walk to the waterfront or dinner on the deck.

local_news.findings: State Route 525 near Alderwood Mall Parkway will undergo overnight closures from Sunday night April 26 through Friday morning May 1 for pavement grinding and repair; only northbound lanes and some ramps will be affected and drivers are advised to expect delays.

A 58-year-old woman was found unresponsive in her cell at the Snohomish County Jail medical housing unit around 7:25 a.m. Wednesday; lifesaving efforts failed and an unexpected fatality review plus Sheriff’s Office death investigation are underway. She had been booked April 20 on suspicion of loitering, drug possession and a Stay Out of Drug Areas violation.

openings.summary: 1.0 FTE continuing contract for 2026-27 school year; requires Spanish proficiency and ELL/ESOL/Bilingual endorsement preferred.
Full-time position starting fall 2026 teaching US History, World History, Civics, and Washington State History.
0.6 FTE long-term substitute May 4–June 15, 2026 for grades 7-8; $28.67–$34.67/hour based on days worked.
Part- or full-time position for 2026-27 school year at Clyde Hill JH/HS; Christian-school environment, salary per schedule.
Anticipated openings for 2026-27; general pool posting open now, specific postings typically go live April–June.
Full-time position in Bothell; secondary ELD endorsement required, salary $75,182–$144,194.
Pool posting for anticipated 2026-27 vacancies; part- or full-time social studies roles district-wide.
Multiple 2026-27 English positions listed; apply through district site or EdJobList/Indeed postings.

career.notes: Most districts are now posting 2026-27 contracts. Edmonds, Lake Washington, and Everett are using general pool postings—apply early to be considered as specific openings arise. Snohomish has an immediate long-term sub opportunity, and Bellevue Christian offers part-time flexibility. All positions require valid WA teaching certificate with appropriate endorsements.

family.choir: Seattle Choral Company accepts applications year-round for all voice sections, with vacancies typically available at season start. Auditions consist of vocalizing, sight-reading, and prepared pieces. Contact via website to schedule. Seattle Pro Musica auditions for 2026-27 season will be held next August—email auditions@seattlepromusica.org to join the mailing list. Northwest Chorale (part of NW Associated Arts) schedules auditions by appointment with Artistic Director Paula Hawkins at 206-719-1179 or prhawkeye@yahoo.com.
family.toddler: Edmonds Library (Sno-Isle) offers weekly Baby Storytime (0-18 mo) Tue 9 & 10 a.m., Toddler Storytime (18-36 mo) Thu 9:30 a.m., and Preschool Storytime (3-5 yr) Thu 10:30 a.m. Imagine Children’s Museum in Everett (15 min drive) is open Tue-Sun 9-4; admission $22, under-2 free—three floors of hands-on exhibits perfect for toddlers. Woodland Park Zoo’s Zoomazium has daily sensory-rich toddler activities including puppet shows and nature play; zoo opens 9:30 a.m. daily.

global_news.findings: President Trump abruptly cancelled a planned US delegation trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, saying envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would be 'wasting too much time'. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi had just left Islamabad after stating Iran was waiting to see if the US was 'truly serious about diplomacy'. The diplomatic breakdown comes as the ceasefire extension expires and tensions remain high over the Strait of Hormuz, with the US maintaining a naval blockade on Iranian oil exports.

Mali's capital Bamako came under coordinated attack by armed groups including al-Qaeda-linked JNIM and Tuareg FLA rebels, described as the largest jihadist assault in years. Explosions and gunfire erupted around Kati military base and Bamako's international airport, with simultaneous attacks reported in Gao, Kidal and Sevare. The US Embassy advised citizens to shelter in place as the military junta claimed to be repelling the attackers.

Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán stepped down from parliament after his Fidesz party suffered a landslide defeat in April elections, ending his 15-year tenure. Orbán will not take up his parliamentary seat as his party transitions to opposition status for the first time since 2010.

Global diplomatic efforts are fraying as the Iran conflict shows no resolution and new crises emerge. The IMF has downgraded global growth forecasts to 3.1% for 2026, citing Middle East conflict impacts. Meanwhile, 266 million people across 47 countries face acute food insecurity, with famines confirmed in Gaza and Sudan - the first time two separate famines have been recorded in a single year.

Russia launched a major overnight assault on Ukraine with over 660 drones and missiles, killing at least seven people including five in Dnipro. Poland activated air defenses as debris fell within its territory, while NATO scrambled RAF Typhoons in response to the 10-hour attack. Ukrainian forces report successfully repelling intensive Russian assaults across Donetsk Oblast.

triadic_ontology.findings: In "Triadic Logic and the Quantum Entanglement of Relations" (2026, Sophia Studies), philosopher-theologian Dr. Elena Vassilaki extends the Karl-Alber series by introducing a non-linear triadic dynamics grounded in quantum perichoresis. She argues that the classical binary of substance–relation collapses when confronted with tripartite quantum correlations, proposing a three-place logical operator that is neither symmetric nor transitive but dynamically entangled. Vassilaki’s key move is to treat the Trinity not as a theological metaphor but as a formal ontological template: Father, Son, and Spirit become placeholders for three mutually indwelling quantum fields whose relational states evolve according to a triadic Schrödinger equation. The paper’s centerpiece is a tensor model showing how perichoretic ‘circumincession’ can be mathematically expressed as a cyclic permutation group that preserves information while allowing ontological novelty—what she calls ‘relational superposition.’ C [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: The autonomous research landscape has exploded with multi-agent systems now capable of end-to-end scientific discovery. Google DeepMind's Deep Research Max, launched this week, runs 160+ searches overnight using Gemini 3.1 Pro to deliver exhaustive reports, while their PaperOrchestra framework autonomously converts raw lab notes into submission-ready manuscripts through five specialized agents. The open-source Sibyl system orchestrates 20+ agents across a 19-stage pipeline that iteratively improves research quality through dual-loop self-evolution. Meanwhile, academic frameworks like ROMA introduce recursive task decomposition for long-horizon reasoning, and Epsilon enforces epistemic integrity through strict separation between experiment design and statistical evaluation. These systems now integrate proprietary data via MCP protocols, generate native visualizations, and achieve 84-93% benchmark scores while maintaining complete audit trails.

uap.findings: The House Oversight subcommittee on National Security held a closed-door UAP briefing on April 24, 2026, but issued no public readout, frustrating transparency advocates. The newly-formed UAP Transparency Caucus, co-chaired by Rep. Moskowitz, convened its first meeting to push for declassification of non-human intelligence programs, citing national security risks from continued secrecy. NASA released an interim update to its independent UAP study, noting progress in standardizing data collection across military sensors and civilian radar networks, though specific findings remain classified pending final review scheduled for late 2026. The Pentagon’s AARO office quietly published an addendum to its 2026 historical report acknowledging "anomalous aerial phenomena" dating to 1945, with redacted sections suggesting ongoing reverse-engineering programs. Meanwhile, leaked documents indicate the Senate Intelligence Committee is drafting legislation to create a civilian review board with secur [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: Woman, 58, died in her cell at Snohomish County Jail | HeraldNet.com
enriched_articles.text:
![Site Logo](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2026/02/HeraldNet2.png)
![](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/themes/2024-cmg/media/img/Search_icon-black.png)

## Woman, 58, died in her cell at Snohomish County Jail

Published 4:18 pm Wednesday, April 22, 2026

**By [Jenna Millikan](https://www.heraldnet.com/author/jenna-millikan/)**

![The Snohomish County Jail is pictured on Thursday, Oct. 26, 2023, in Everett, Washington. (Ryan Berry / The Herald)](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2026/04/42139406_web1_L1-SnoCoJail-EDH-231026-FS.jpg?w=1024)

The Snohomish County Jail is pictured on Thursday, Oct. 26, 2023, in Everett, Washington. (Ryan Berry / The Herald)

EVERETT — An inmate died inside her cell Wednesday morning at Snohomish County Jail.

Around 7:25 a.m. Wednesday, a corrections deputy found a 58-year-old woman unresponsive inside her cell at the Snohomish County Jail during routine checks, according to a Snohomish County Sheriff’s Office press release. She was housed in the jail’s medical housing unit.

Corrections staff declared a medical emergency and began lifesaving measures, but resuscitation attempts were unsuccessful, the relea [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war - BBC
enriched_articles.text:
# Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war

![](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/bbcdotcom/web/20260409-151157-6d668e92bf-web-3.1.0-1/grey-placeholder.png)
![Getty Images US President Donald Trump speaks to reporters before boarding Air Force One in Palm Beach, Florida.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/8b84/live/6b377100-40e5-11f1-ac78-2112837ce2aa.jpg.webp)

Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US officials to Pakistan for talks on the Iran war on Saturday, shortly after Tehran's delegation had left Islamabad.

The US president said special envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner would be wasting "too much time", adding that if Iran wanted to talk "all they have to do is call".

Earlier, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi held talks with mediator Pakistan, saying afterwards he had shared Iran's position on ending the war but was yet to see whether the US was "truly serious about diplomacy".

Diplomatic efforts have stalled despite Trump's extension of a ceasefire that had been due to expire on 22 April to allow talks to continue.

Both sides have been locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, with Iran restricting passage th [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: Armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali - BBC
enriched_articles.text:
# Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali

![](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/bbcdotcom/web/20260409-151157-6d668e92bf-web-3.1.0-1/grey-placeholder.png)
![Reuters A man sits on his knees in a brick building looking through a window](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/c528/live/7d4de040-4090-11f1-bd52-e755d604ece4.jpg.webp)

Explosions and sustained gunfire have been reported in Mali's capital Bamako as armed groups launch apparently coordinated attacks across the country.

In a statement on Saturday the military said "fighting is ongoing", adding "our defence and security forces are currently engaged in repelling the attackers".

Witnesses have told the Reuters news agency of explosions and gunfire around the Kati military base, a major installation outside the capital. Soldiers have been deployed to block off roads in the area.

There are also reports of attacks in Gao and Kidal in the north and Sevare in central Mali, in what an analyst describes as the largest jihadist attack in years.

One resident, who was travelling back to Bamako from Ethiopia, told the BBC that all flights into the city were cancelled early on Saturday. It is  [TRUNCATED]
